title: dryopteris squamigera
intro: Dryopteris squamigera, also known as the hairy shield fern, is a species of fern that is native to Asia, characterized by its hairy fronds and small, lance-shaped leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe wounds and reduce swelling. The health benefits of Dryopteris squamigera include its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce fever, and aid in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and pneumonia. Dryopteris squamigera can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion for medicinal use, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
